message:
Include full attribute descriptions in OCSF documentation

Use the complete attribute descriptions from the OCSF schema instead
of just captions. This provides users with detailed information about
each attribute, matching the official OCSF site experience.

- Update object attribute descriptions to use full schema descriptions
- Update class attribute descriptions to use full schema descriptions
- Update profile attribute descriptions to use full schema descriptions
- Clean HTML tags from descriptions using existing cleanDescription helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch: @@ -6,12 +6,21 @@ description: Reference documentation for the Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework import { CardGrid, LinkCard } from "@astrojs/starlight/components"; import ocsfLogo from "../../../../assets/ocsf.svg"; - + This reference provides comprehensive documentation for the [Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework (OCSF)](https://ocsf.io), an open standard for normalizing security telemetry across tools and vendors. +:::tip[Why embed the OCSF docs?] +We publish this OCSF reference within the Tenzir documentation because our format +is optimized for AI consumption. Every page is available as raw Markdown by +appending `.md` to any URL, and all schema elements are fully cross-referenced +with navigable links. This makes it easy for language models to understand and +traverse the OCSF schema programmatically. Download an AI-optimized archive at +our [release page](https://github.com/tenzir/docs/releases/tag/latest). +::: + ## Resources @@ -37,6 +46,29 @@ for normalizing security telemetry across tools and vendors. /> +## Versions + +| Version | Classes | Objects | Profiles | Types | +| ------------------------------ | ------- | ------- | -------- | ----- | +| [1.7.0](/reference/ocsf/1-7-0) | 83 | 170 | 12 | 24 | +| [1.6.0](/reference/ocsf/1-6-0) | 82 | 167 | 12 | 24 | +| [1.5.0](/reference/ocsf/1-5-0) | 81 | 161 | 12 | 24 | +| [1.4.0](/reference/ocsf/1-4-0) | 79 | 142 | 12 | 22 | +| [1.3.0](/reference/ocsf/1-3-0) | 72 | 121 | 10 | 22 | +| [1.2.0](/reference/ocsf/1-2-0) | 65 | 111 | 9 | 22 | +| [1.1.0](/reference/ocsf/1-1-0) | 50 | 106 | 8 | 22 | +| [1.0.0](/reference/ocsf/1-0-0) | 36 | 84 | 6 | 22 | + +:::tip[Why embed the OCSF docs?] +We publish this OCSF reference within the Tenzir documentation because our format +is optimized for AI consumption. This makes it easy for language models to understand and +traverse the OCSF schema programmatically in a compact representation.
